_comment: Eval template for autoresearch skill. Copy this to ~/.claude/skills/[your-skill]/eval/eval.json and fill in your test cases.
description: Brief description of what this skill does and what we're testing

test_cases.prompt: The exact user input to feed into the skill — write it as if you're the user
test_cases.context: Optional: describe what scenario this is testing (not fed to skill, just for documentation)

assertions.check: Output is at least 100 words (not a stub)
Final line is a concrete action or takeaway, not a question or open-ended reflection

test_cases.prompt: Second test prompt — cover a different input type or edge case
test_cases.context: Testing edge case: very short/simple input

assertions.check: Starts with a strong opening sentence (not 'I' as the first word)
Output contains at least one specific number, statistic, or concrete example
First line is a standalone complete sentence (not a heading)

test_cases.prompt: Third test prompt — test the most common use case

assertions.check: Does not use the word 'leverage' as a verb
Output has a clear 3-part structure: setup/problem, middle/solution, ending/result

_assertion_types_reference.not_contains: Pass if output does NOT contain 'value' string
_assertion_types_reference.regex: Pass if output matches 'pattern' (JavaScript regex syntax)
_assertion_types_reference.line_check: Evaluate first or last line; 'line' is 'first' or 'last', 'check' is evaluated as true/false
_assertion_types_reference.custom: Claude evaluates 'check' description as binary true/false about the output

_good_assertions: First line is a standalone sentence (not part of a paragraph)
Contains at least one specific number or statistic
Final line is NOT a question
Does not contain 'In conclusion' or 'In summary'
Does not use passive voice more than once
